commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Discord, Slack, and Telegram ack-reaction gates, docs, tests, and `CHANGELOG.md` so `messages.ackReactionScope: "all"` can send initial ack reactions for ambient `room_event` messages while lifecycle status reactions stay suppressed.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +133, Docs +9. Total +153 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main and `v2026.5.27` suppress room-event ack reactions before or after the shared `all` scope gate in the affected channels, and current tests encode the old quiet behavior. I did not run live transport proof in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the PR has a protected maintainer label, changes compatibility-visible channel reaction behavior, and still needs real behavior proof or maintainer override.

target.title: [codex] Honor all ack scope for room events
target.body:
## Summary
- lets Discord, Slack, and Telegram send the configured initial ack reaction for ambient `room_event` messages when `messages.ackReactionScope` is `"all"`
- keeps lifecycle status reactions suppressed for room events unless that behavior is changed separately
- documents the cross-channel scope semantics and adds a changelog entry

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#87368.

## Notes
Review flagged that the original Discord-only change diverged from Slack and Telegram. The follow-up commit aligns the sibling chat plugins instead: non-forcing scopes such as `"group-all"` still keep unmentioned room events reaction-silent, while the explicit `"all"` scope opts into a plain receipt ack across Discord, Slack, and Telegram.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts -t 'room events'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts -t 'room events|message-tool-only'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.reactions.test.ts -t 'room events|status reactions'`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md docs/channels/slack.md docs/channels/telegram.md extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.reactions.test.ts`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `git diff --check`

Behavior addressed: `messages.ackReactionScope: "all"` now means the initial ack can be sent for quiet room events on Discord, Slack, and Telegram; lifecycle status reactions remain suppressed for room events.
Real environment tested: local macOS source worktree at `upstream/main`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest, formatter, docs MDX, and diff whitespace checks above.
Evidence after fix: Discord room-event tests passed with 3 matching tests; Slack room-event/message-tool-only tests passed with 4 matching tests; Telegram room-event/status-reaction tests passed with 3 matching tests.
Observed result after fix: non-forcing scopes still suppress room-event reactions; `"all"` sends the configured initial ack for the supported chat plugins.
What was not tested: live Discord/Slack/Telegram workspace delivery.
